top_issues.issue: Indecent Exposure: private methods (_authEndpoint, _loginEndpoint, _revokeEndpoint, _notifyAndResolve, _resolveOAuth) exported onto public Connection prototype
Primitive Obsession / Data Clump: pervasive mutable opts-bag pattern carries credentials, HTTP verbs, payloads, retry state, and feature flags through every layer
God Module: 503 lines, 30 exports combining 8 unrelated concerns (CRUD, metadata, blob, query, search, URL access, Apex REST, streaming)
Duplicated Code: identical AbortSignal.timeout/merge block repeated in _apiAuthRequest (lines 100-106) and _apiRequest (lines 139-145)
Duplicated Code: multipart-or-JSON body selection logic duplicated in insert() and update()
Dead Write / Status Variable: opts._refreshResult assigned but never read; opts._retryCount embeds flow-control state in the data bag
Lazy Element: _resolveOAuth is a one-line Promise.resolve wrapper with no added behaviour, exported publicly and tested directly
Feature Envy: multipart() reaches into Record for type, fileName, payload, and body instead of Record owning its multipart representation
Inappropriate Intimacy: tests access _fields, _changed, _previous, and _getPayload directly, coupling tests to private implementation details
Required Setup Code: nforce.createConnection() with full boilerplate repeated verbatim in 17 individual test cases
